mnemonics: CHAINSAW: Chunking, Hashing, Indexing, Numexpr, String dtypes, Avoid loops, Weave (Cython) for high-performance Pandas.
keyPoints: Use vectorized operations instead of iterative loops (e.g., apply, map) for massive speed improvements.
Choose efficient data types (e.g., category for strings, int/float subtypes) to reduce memory usage.
Process large datasets in chunks with `chunksize` or `iterator` to avoid memory overload.
commonMistakes: Using `df.iterrows()` or `df.apply()` with custom functions for large datasets, which is extremely slow.
Loading an entire massive CSV into memory without considering chunking or efficient data types first.
Not using categorical data types for string columns with low cardinality, missing out on significant memory savings.
